evals.prompt: I want to add a new project called data_fetcher under projects/. It fetches data from a REST API and saves it as JSON. Main class should be Fetcher. No dependencies yet.
evals.expected_output: Scaffolded projects/data_fetcher/ with main.py (pure argparse + call), libs/__init__.py, libs/fetcher.py with typed Fetcher skeleton (pass bodies only), README.md with real content, root requirements.txt updated
assertions: all-required-files-created: main.py, requirements.txt, libs/__init__.py, libs/fetcher.py, README.md all exist under projects/data_fetcher/
methods-are-skeleton: every method body in libs/fetcher.py contains only 'pass' — no real logic, no urllib/requests calls, no file I/O, no data processing
no-logic-in-main: main.py contains only argparse setup and a single call into libs/ — no print statements, no conditional branching on results, no business logic
class-has-type-hints: Fetcher.__init__ has typed parameters; all methods have typed return values
readme-has-real-content: README.md has a usage section with 'make run PROJECT=projects/data_fetcher' and a project structure section

evals.prompt: Create a new tool at tools/slack_notifier that sends Slack messages when CI pipelines finish. Main class: SlackNotifier. Dependencies: requests, slack-sdk.
evals.expected_output: Scaffolded tools/slack_notifier/ with skeleton SlackNotifier class (pass bodies), both deps in requirements.txt, root requirements.txt updated, informative README
assertions: all-required-files-created: main.py, requirements.txt, libs/__init__.py, libs/slack_notifier.py (or similar), README.md all exist under tools/slack_notifier/
methods-are-skeleton: every method body in libs/ contains only 'pass' — no actual Slack API calls, no message building logic, no HTTP requests
no-logic-in-main: main.py contains only argparse setup and a single call into libs/ — no business logic or multi-step orchestration
class-has-type-hints: SlackNotifier class has typed __init__ and typed method signatures

evals.prompt: Let's build something new — a log analyzer that reads server logs and produces a summary report. Put it under projects/log_analyzer. I'm thinking a class called Analyzer with a parse() and summarize() method.
evals.expected_output: Scaffolded projects/log_analyzer/ with Analyzer class having parse() and summarize() as typed stubs (pass only), thin main.py, informative README, root requirements.txt updated
assertions: all-required-files-created: main.py, requirements.txt, libs/__init__.py, libs/analyzer.py (or similar), README.md all exist under projects/log_analyzer/
parse-and-summarize-present: Analyzer class has both parse() and summarize() methods with typed return values
methods-are-skeleton: parse() and summarize() both contain only 'pass' — no regex, no file reading, no data structures, no logic
no-logic-in-main: main.py contains only argparse setup and a single call into libs/ — no output formatting, no exit code logic
class-has-type-hints: Analyzer.__init__ has typed parameters; parse() and summarize() have typed return values
